text.title: BA Requirements Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding this methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale; the skip-this-methodology rule is the authoritative stop signal.

rule.statement: Produce the spec artefact required by this methodology with every required field of the JSON Schema in 02-output-contract.xml populated and non-null.
rule.rationale: Missing required fields propagate to downstream consumers as silent gaps; the validator script enforces this rule at CI time.

rule.statement: Apply the decision tree in 06-decision-tree.xml before authoring; record the leaf reached so reviewers can replay the routing.
rule.rationale: Skipping the decision tree leads to misapplied methodologies (e.g. running deep procedure on light scope); recording the leaf makes the application auditable.

rule.statement: Detect and prevent the antipattern 'unversioned-changes': Enforce semantic versioning on every requirement; bump version on any edit; keep diff history.
rule.rationale: Root cause: Author treats requirements as living documents without baselining.

rule.statement: Detect and prevent the antipattern 'rubber-stamp-approval': Force a written rationale field on every decision; reject empty-rationale approvals.
rule.rationale: Root cause: Approver does not read the impact assessment.

rule.statement: Detect and prevent the antipattern 'orphan-changes': Validate every CR.req_id against the register; reject orphans.
rule.rationale: Root cause: CR is created from a downstream artefact (test case, design doc) without linking back.

rule.statement: When the decision tree resolves to skip-this-methodology, halt authoring; do NOT emit a partial artefact and do NOT proceed downstream with this methodology's outputs.
rule.rationale: Partial artefacts produced after a skip signal pollute downstream consumers and waste reviewer cycles; the skip leaf is an authoritative stop signal.
